displayName.text: Beer Spa Bernard Prague (in Majestic Plaza hotel)

text.text:
We had an unforgettable experience at Bernard Beer Spa! 🍺✨

From the moment we arrived, the staff was so friendly and welcoming.

The interior is cozy and beautifully designed, and everything feels private and very clean.

The beer spa itself was amazing — relaxing in the warm hop-infused bath while sipping unlimited Bernard beer was truly unique (and a dream come true for my beer-loving husband 🧡).

The private room also included a sauna and a comfortable hay bed, which made the whole experience even more special.

We also enjoyed a massage afterward — the perfect way to end such a relaxing and fun spa day.

Definitely one of the highlights of our trip to Prague!

Highly recommend this experience to anyone looking for something unique, relaxing, and memorable.

text.text: We had the best time here! Highly recommend if you find yourself in prague. The beer spa is an iconic experience you just have to try.

text.text: Best beer spa experience ever. If you are in Prague definitely check them out! We had a wonderful time and everyone was super nice.

originalText.text: Best beer spa experience ever. If you are in Prague definitely check them out! We had a wonderful time and everyone was super nice.

text.text:
Lovely place, very relaxing and perfect for couples. The staff were very kind and welcoming.There was one thing i was really disappointed with.

I went in my new blue bikini, but I wasn't warned that apparently the bath might stain swimsuits. I waited until I came home to put the bikini in the washing machine to see if it really did stain, and yes, it did. My brand new bikini that i brought with me still with its tag is stained a darker green-blue than when i bought it :/. I am very disappointed and would like to warn other guests about this.

text.text:
We took an hour of beer spa and an hour of massage. I want to say that we left absolutely delighted with the beer bath!
But I don't recommend wasting time and money on a massage. It wasn't a massage, just light stroking. The massage was a disappointment. Several times we asked her to make it stronger, but you can't really call it a massage. It was just light stroking all over the body.

authorAttributions.displayName: Beer Spa Bernard Prague (in Majestic Plaza hotel)

text.text:
I had such a wonderful and memorable experience at The Original Beer Spa in Prague, and they truly made my birthday feel extra special. From the moment I arrived, everything felt well thought out, relaxing, and unique.

Aneta was incredibly helpful and welcoming — she took the time to explain the entire process clearly, which made the experience even more enjoyable and stress-free. Once in the spa, I was able to fully relax and reset, soaking in the warm beer bath and just enjoying the peaceful atmosphere.

After the soak, I spent time laying down in the hay bed, which was such a calming and restorative experience and the perfect way to unwind. The entire visit felt indulgent yet soothing, and it was exactly what I needed.

If you’re visiting Prague and looking for a unique, relaxing experience — especially to celebrate a special occasion — I highly recommend The Original Beer Spa. It was the perfect way to unwind, and I’d absolutely do it again!

text.text: This was such an incredible and unique experience! The atmosphere at Original Beer Spa is so relaxing, and soaking in the beer bath was the highlight of my trip. A huge shoutout to Sasha for being so welcoming and professional—she really made sure we had everything we needed. Highly recommend this to anyone visiting!

originalText.text: This was such an incredible and unique experience! The atmosphere at Original Beer Spa is so relaxing, and soaking in the beer bath was the highlight of my trip. A huge shoutout to Sasha for being so welcoming and professional—she really made sure we had everything we needed. Highly recommend this to anyone visiting!

text.text: The Original Beer Spa is a great place to relax, unwind, drink beer, and partake in a unique experience. It is the kind of place that immediately makes you realize you are doing something memorable that you cannot easily replicate anywhere else. This was actually one of the best things we did in Prague. The staff are great and genuinely want you to enjoy yourself, without making anything feel rushed or scripted. The lighting makes it great for pictures inside as well, creating a warm atmosphere that feels both cozy and elevated. They have showers, which is nice, but they suggest you keep the beer on you to preserve the elements for your skin, which makes the experience feel intentional rather than just novelty. It is rare to find an activity that is both relaxing and genuinely different, especially in a city filled with so many tourist options. Regardless, I will gladly be coming back next time I am in Prague.

text.text: We had the best experience at this beer spa for our anniversary! Everything was super clean and prepared, beer, bathtub, atmosphere - everything was perfect. Separate huge thank you to our host Sasha, who made our visit as comfortable as it could get, explained everything carefully and welcomed us with her open heart!❤️ A genuinely 10/10 experience, would recommend!!

editorialSummary.text: Seasonal waterside sports facility offering kayak & SUP rentals, plus croquet & a party raft.

text.text: This is an open-air swimming spot not far from the center. There’s a small pool for young children, as well as some sprinklers and water features. You can also swim in the river, where there’s a pontoon for entering the water but be aware that it gets deep very quickly. Even on a 35°C day the water stays quite cool. It’s easy to find both shade and sunny areas to lie down. The place also has a sports zone with volleyball, changing rooms, toilets, and spots where you can buy food and drinks. Overall, it’s a pretty good and affordable place to spend a hot day - around 120 CZK , but can be pretty crowded.

I saw the lady charging someone else 100 for half an hour ( 4€ ) either she gave me discount or she overcharged him or I misheard

text.text:
Nice to relax on a very hot summer's day, 10 minutes from the city centre.  Take a dip in the river, play ping-pong or volley ball.
Plenty of places with nice food and cold drinks. (Beer).  A hot day well spent if you go there.  Especially after a long party day.

displayName.text: The Spa at Mandarin Oriental, Prague

text.text: We had an amazing spa day with Lucy and Katarina. Almost 5 hours of couples treatments that started with a steam bath for 30 minutes then foot bath then 1.5 hours of massage that was personalized for each of us in a shared room. We then walked across the courtyard for an exceptional dinner at thr hotel restaurant. Definitely one of the best spa experiences that we have ever had! Highly recommended. Thank you to all the staff who made this day such a memorable experience on our trip to Prague.

We then walked across the courtyard for an exceptional dinner at thr hotel restaurant. Definitely one of the best spa experiences that we have ever had! Highly recommended. Thank you to all the staff who made this day such a memorable experience on our trip to Prague.

will definitely come back

text.text: The calmness and possibility to book private slots (for steam bath and jacuzzi), great products for all treatments, also super professionalism and client centric behavior of staff. Overall very relaxing experience. Still our favorite spa in Prague.

originalText.text: The calmness and possibility to book private slots (for steam bath and jacuzzi), great products for all treatments, also super professionalism and client centric behavior of staff. Overall very relaxing experience. Still our favorite spa in Prague.

text.text:
I recently had an amazing massage with Ingrid. She is the best! Her magical hands made me feel completely relaxed, and I loved choosing my favorite scent for the massage. ☺️

A big thank you to the receptionist, whose name I think is Martina, for being so kind and welcoming. The entire spa team was fantastic and made the whole experience perfect from start to finish.

I highly recommend visiting Ingrid and the team for a fantastic massage that will leave you feeling refreshed!  🥰❤️

text.text:
I visited the spa yesterday for a two-hour treatment Bohemian Meadow. While the massage itself was good and I enjoyed the technique, the overall experience fell far below the standards expected from the Mandarin Oriental.

Several issues stood out:
•	Fake flowers cheapened the atmosphere. A five-star resort should not rely on faux décor.
•	I was not provided slippers upon arrival and had to request them. The relaxation area, which used to offer fresh fruit, now only had water, tea, and a few nuts - hardly the elevated experience expected.
•	I booked a two-hour session, yet the therapist started late (maybe about 5 min), explanations took additional time (even though I filled put the request form beforehand), and when I had to use the restroom - located inconveniently far from the treatment rooms back in the changing room - that time was deducted from my session rather than adjusted accordingly.
•	The changing room amenities were noticeably cheap, featuring basic, drugstore-brand products like Rexona deodorant and lacking essentials such as period pads and disposable hairbrushes (women would understand how important it is to have those). A spa of this caliber should offer high-end products in line with its reputation.
•	No one asked for feedback or offered a survey, which is surprising for a luxury establishment…

The most disappointing part was the treatment itself. I specifically booked the Bohemian Meadow for its final scalp treatment, yet it was rushed to just five minutes at the very end due to time constraints.

At $300 per session, these details matter. As a returning local guest, this experience was disappointing, and I hope the Spa Director takes this feedback seriously.

text.text: Amazing experience! 10/10! I will definitely recommend this place for both spa and beer lovers. Aneta is a fantastic hostess - she showed us a very good and comfortable time! So nice to talk to. Call for Aneta and have yourself an experience to remember for a lifetime!

text.text: Amazing experience!  Solo traveler, got my own room.  It was large, perfectly dry and clean, and so cozy.  Sasha at the front desk was friendly and walked me through the process.  Highly recommend.

originalText.text: Amazing experience!  Solo traveler, got my own room.  It was large, perfectly dry and clean, and so cozy.  Sasha at the front desk was friendly and walked me through the process.  Highly recommend.

text.text:
Yes, it’s a bit of a tourist trap—but honestly, it’s one that’s worth experiencing while in Prague. The novelty of soaking in a beer-filled tub, sipping unlimited draft beer, and just kicking back is unlike anything else. I went with my husband, and we each got our own tub, which made it feel extra relaxing and personal. I could also see this being a super fun outing for a small group of friends.

If I had one regret, it’s that we only booked a one-hour session. It wasn’t quite enough time to fully enjoy the soak, take some photos, sip beer, and unwind. If you can, I highly recommend booking a longer session.

Arrive about 10 minutes early to allow time for check-in—everything was smooth, and the staff was pleasant at the start. However, the tone changed a bit at the end. We were notified right when our time was up, and within a minute of heading to the restroom, someone was already knocking to see if I was done. It felt very rushed and left us scrambling to get dressed and out the door, which put a bit of a damper on the experience.

On a brighter note, the bread and tofu spread they served were surprisingly delicious—possibly even better than the beer!

Overall, it’s a unique and memorable experience.

text.text: What an awesome experience. A very warm welcome by the two lovely ladies Aneta and Anastasia. The bathing experience in a very cosy room with unlimited, tasty tap beer and a hay bed was very relaxing. 10/10 we will definitely come back 😊

text.text:
Review: Original Beer Spa – A One-Time Experience Worth Knowing About

We visited the Original Beer Spa over the weekend — a group of four guys curious about what the hype was all about. While the name might evoke visions of soaking in beer, the reality is a little different. The tubs aren’t filled with actual beer, but rather a mix of water and beer-related ingredients like hops and brewer’s yeast, which they claim are beneficial for the skin.

The experience starts with a quick introduction from the staff. They explain the setup and set a 51-minute timer for your session. Once the timer goes off, you’re expected to shower and head out. The room itself is quite warm, almost sauna-like, and the sturdy wooden tub maintains a steady temperature of around 37°C with bubbling jets to keep things relaxing.

You are offered unlimited free beer during the session, but realistically, not many people drink much in that one hour — I don’t drink myself, so it didn’t matter to me. Still, it might be a perk for beer enthusiasts.

As for the staff, they were functional — they answered questions and did their job, but don’t expect warm hospitality or enthusiastic smiles. They’re polite, but the overall vibe was more transactional than welcoming.

The spa is decent overall, and it might be worth trying once just for the novelty, but for the price, I personally didn’t feel it offered great value. It’s not something I’d return to — I’ve had my experience, and that’s enough.

Tip: Make sure to keep pictures of the entrance and location, especially if you’re visiting for the first time. It helps to know exactly where you’re going, as it’s easy to miss or walk past it without realizing.

editorialSummary.text: Casual quarters in a bustling hotel featuring a sprawling water park, a restaurant & bars.

text.text:
Me and my boyfriend had a two-day stay from Sunday to Monday. I have a very mixed review.
First of all, pluses:
- Good value for money, room in a hotel + breakfast + 2 days of aquapark for 2 adults costed us 127 euros.
- Very clean aquapark, especially toilets.
- Big choice of pools, there is even an area with 180 cm depth.
- Pool with bar, although the choice of cocktails isn't huge.
- Breakfast was decent and room also fine.

Cons:
- both me and my boyfriend had our heads hit when using "Crazy tube" slide. Nothing too bad, but we were scared and complaining. The slide itself is fun, but why do you position it as a "family friendly" when its size (at the end) is clearly not adult friendly?! I am 165 cm height and I feel like even for me this slide is dangerous.
- I haven't seen lifeguards next to a 180 cm deep pool. As well as I haven't seen aquapark workers next to entry to the slides (all of this as of Monday December 1st). I feel like at least "magic tube" and "spacebowl" should have somebody checking age, helping out, ensuring distance. After seeing this and having our heads hit we haven't returned to slides at all even though we usually love them in aquaparks.

text.text: Day visit to aqua Park. Lots of slides, 13&11yr old loved it. Rapids was closed which was disappointing as no notification on website when booking, although we would have still visited and had a great time. Lunch was also very nice and good selection.

text.text:
Wow. Thats how my kids kept describing it even after we left.

Easy access by public transportation.
Family price ticket for 2 adults and 2 children.
Lockers, showers, swim clothes dryer, hair dryers, in water adults bar, 3 halls filled with water playgrounds  pools and toys, wave pool, dozen of slides and so much more..

Perfectly constructed. Brilliantly maintained. A perfect place to go to.

text.text: Very nice experience. Excecutive room with its perks - a nice addition to the aquapalace waterpark. The water park itself was okay, i liked the wild river slide, although im kinda sad the outdoors part was closed! Also water was a bit too cold for being outside in November. Might come back, might not! I think I liked the Aqualand Moravia a bit more, as their saunas just cannot be topped!

text.text: I went to the aquapark with my kids 12 and 14. We had a blast there, and really enjoyed it. We took the full day pass, and I took the sauna access which is a must visit. It was December, so the nice experience was going into the lazy river outdoors, being warm and feeling the cold air on your face. They have around 12 slides, pools, outdoor space. Changing rooms are super clean, hairdryer available, machine to dry your swimsuit. Overall, we loved it and would come back if we visit prague again.

text.text:
In November 2025, I attended the incredible immersive GOODWILL SHOW NOANNE in Prague and it was one of the most powerful experiences I’ve had in a long time. Everything was thought through down to the smallest detail, from the invitation emails to the very last second of the show. 10,000% immersion ✨

The actors’ performance and the live vocals were outstanding it gave me goosebumps and truly made me look inward. Highly recommended!

originalText.text:
In November 2025, I attended the incredible immersive GOODWILL SHOW NOANNE in Prague and it was one of the most powerful experiences I’ve had in a long time. Everything was thought through down to the smallest detail, from the invitation emails to the very last second of the show. 10,000% immersion ✨

The actors’ performance and the live vocals were outstanding it gave me goosebumps and truly made me look inward. Highly recommended!

text.text: We went to the GOODWILL show premier, and had an amazing time. The experience was very immersive, with dark, heavy, mysterious atmosphere built with the masks, dress code, music, decorations and even the specially developed cocktails. Noanne singing is incredible and the interactive elements of the show made it even better and more captivating. Will definitely recommend my friends to visit the next show (if they can get hold of the fast selling tickets😉)

originalText.text: We went to the GOODWILL show premier, and had an amazing time. The experience was very immersive, with dark, heavy, mysterious atmosphere built with the masks, dress code, music, decorations and even the specially developed cocktails. Noanne singing is incredible and the interactive elements of the show made it even better and more captivating. text.text:
We had the absolute pleasure of seeing Octopussy in Prague as a gift for a friend, and it turned into an unforgettable night for all of us! From the moment we stepped into the intimate, vintage-style theatre, we were transported to another world. The atmosphere was electric! cozy, glamorous, and full of anticipation. The bar was charming and well-stocked with delicious, reasonably priced drinks that added perfectly to the experience.

Every single performer was professional, captivating, and undeniably talented. We were completely mesmerized from start to finish. The show struck the perfect balance between classic burlesque elegance and bold modern extravagance. You can truly tell these performers are the best of the best.

We’ve never seen anything quite like it, and we’re already talking about coming back. If you want to travel back in time and experience authentic burlesque with a dazzling contemporary flair, Theater Royale by Burlesque Prague is the place to be. Thank you for an unforgettable night – we’ll definitely be seeing you again!

text.text:
I like that it’s both classy and not imposing.
You can dive into the special atmosphere without being judged by the staff.

originalText.text:
I like that it’s both classy and not imposing.
You can dive into the special atmosphere without being judged by the staff.

text.text:
Goodwill show was unreal. The atmosphere was intense, immersive, and felt completely different from anything I’ve experienced before.
The world, the aesthetics, the attention to detail - it all felt deeply thought-through. Being there honestly felt like being part of a very closed, almost secret community, and that was a huge wow.
For me, it was like a perfect mix of everything I love: Art Deco, Orwell, peak-era Tim Burton, and A Cure for Wellness.
Smart, visually powerful, and unforgettable. This is not just a show - it’s an experience.

originalText.text:

For me, it was like a perfect mix of everything I love: Art Deco, Orwell, peak-era Tim Burton, and A Cure for Wellness.
Smart, visually powerful, and unforgettable. This is not just a show - it’s an experience.

editorialSummary.text: Theater-turned-stylish boutique hotel with fine dining, polished lounges & a rooftop terrace.

text.text: This is almost one of those places that you might want to keep to yourself so it isn't overrun with bad tourists, but that would not be fair to the owners and staff. We researched many different hotels on a recent trip to Prague and are SO thankful we stayed here. It is in a quiet part of Prague but still close to everything. Nice restaurants, tram line. The food was outstanding. Rooms very tastefully decorated and quiet. We frequent 4 and 5 star hotels worldwide and without a doubt, this place was a favorite. Ironically, we left the hotel after the first few days for a planned trip to Vienna for a weekend, and stayed at a Hilton there, and could not wait to come back to the Aria Hotel as the Hilton seemed too corporate and sterile. If we happen to find ourselves in Prague again, I would stay here again. My only regret was not checking out their sister hotel (Golden Well) as a backup.

If we happen to find ourselves in Prague again, I would stay here again. My only regret was not checking out their sister hotel (Golden Well) as a backup.

text.text:
One of the best options to stay in Mala Strana, great design and detail, the common living room glass ceiling is beautiful!
Also the restaurant in the rooftop is delicious and has a Great view from Prague and the Castle!

originalText.text:
One of the best options to stay in Mala Strana, great design and detail, the common living room glass ceiling is beautiful!
Also the restaurant in the rooftop is delicious and has a Great view from Prague and the Castle!

text.text:
The superb location, the quietness of the room and its excellent cleanliness makes Aria hotel very high value for money. We are glad that we chose this hotel for our vacation and it is defiantly deserve a very high score.
Despite the hotel is very close to Charles bridge and Prague's castle, it was very quite. We walked to all the attractions easily every day. There are many excellent restaurants and bars around Aria hotel and it was easy for us to find something to eat in any hour. Our room view was the beautiful Vrtba Garden and there is a free access to it from the hotel.
The Concierge team (especially "Henry" who showed high care about the hotel and its guests) & reception was very helpful and answered every question we had very quickly and professionally. We also experienced amazing organized hiking day trip from the hotel to Saxon-Czech area, which is highly recommended. We appreciated the hotel's beautiful musical design and felt connected to its musical classical atmosphere. The hotel gifted us with premium experience in the Coda restaurant, which was exceptional and unforgettable.

We are very sorry to write that the breakfast fell very short from our 5 stars expectations. The variety of food and the amount of food were consistently very poor. Frankly, 5 stars hotel cannot afford serving a glass of vegetables and very few portions of cheese and salmon every single day. The breads at the buffet were always dry. When we ordered pancakes without syrups, they were very dry and the taste was dreadful. The staff at the breakfast was inconsistent, at the first 2-3 days there was an unfriendly woman who made everyone nervous. At the other days, the atmosphere was much lighter, and the staff was mostly very kind to us. We would like to mention a guy named Tom who we met also in the Coda restaurant. Tom's presence made everything smooth and calm. He made our birthday celebration experience much better at the breakfast and made us so happy. Thank you Tom! :)
Other small things that could be better:
1. Not enough lights in the room
2. High speed charging power sockets located close to the floor and only works if the light is on. Therefore, we had to charge our phone on the floor and with the light on.
3. We were told that currently the air conditioner couldn't cool the room and in some nights it was a bit too warm.
4. We were told that the odor extraction fan was turned off because one guy from higher floor said he hates fans. The odors from the toilet was unpleasant sometimes and there was no way to control it.
5. Internet frequently stuck when we tried to watch movies, but after couple of min it was smooth. Overall it was bearable.
6. The document that states the hotel charges upon check out was extremely difficult to understand.
7. Some of the staff didn't speak English well and there were some slight miscommunications.

text.text:
My name is Mark Lisnow, myself and my wife Carole Lucas just finished spending a month at the Aria Hotel. It was the most enjoyable experience we have ever had in a hotel. We are both retired and seasoned travelers and have been staying in hotel all over the World for the past 60 years.. The Aria is the best hotel we have ever experienced. It was highly recommended by a friend of ours who has lived in Prague his whole life. The hotel is professionally operated. The front desk is extremely professional and could not do enough for us. The morning breakfast is sensational and usually included in the price of the room. The wait staff is incredible. The conceige service is knowledgeable and always there to help you navigate your day.
A testament to the hotel is the owner and his wife are there every day working hard to make sure your experience is outstanding
I highly recommend this hotel.. also an outstanding location literally around the corner from the Charles Bridge

text.text: Stayed four nights at this hotel. Its location is close to several landmarks, restaurants and public transportation. It is well appointed, attended by helpful and friendly staff. The included breakfast is excellent, with made to order items in addition to the offered buffet.

originalText.text: Stayed four nights at this hotel. Its location is close to several landmarks, restaurants and public transportation. It is well appointed, attended by helpful and friendly staff. The included breakfast is excellent, with made to order items in addition to the offered buffet.

text.text: Our favorite wellness right under Prague Castle, ideal for relaxing after a city tour. Space rental is only possible privately. I personally recommend the steam bath in a natural stone cave. The wellness also offers a variety of massages and treatments. The atmosphere is cozy, the staff is very friendly. Definitely worth a visit.

originalText.text: Náš oblíbený wellness přímo pod Pražským hradem, ideální pro odpočinek po prohlídce města. Pronájem prostor je možný jenom privátně. Osobně doporučuji parní lázeň v přírodní kamenné jeskyni. Wellness nabízí také řadu masáží a procedur. Atmosféra je útulná, personál je velmi příjemný. Rozhodně stojí za návštěvu.

text.text: My wife and I expected anything, but the reality exceeded all our expectations! Perfect care, environment and service! We can't wait to come back and try other procedures. Thank you very much!

originalText.text: Čekali jsme s manželkou cokoliv, ale skutečnost předčila veškeré naše představy! Dokonalá péče, prostředí i služby! Už se nemůžeme dočkat, až se vrátíme a zkusíme další procedury. Moc děkujeme!

text.text: Wellness, where you want to return. It has its own special charm, it is possible to enjoy it even as a non-accommodated hotel guest. The steam in the cave is the best :-). Next time I will definitely include a massage in my relaxation. Pleasant staff, great comfort.

originalText.text: Welness, kam se chcete vracet. Má své osobité kouzlo, je možné si ho užít i jako neubytovaný host hotelu. Pára v jeskyni nejvíc :-). Příště určitě zahrnu do relaxu nějakou masáž. Příjemný personál, velká pohoda.

text.text: I am very satisfied with my visit to the wellness center. The environment is really beautiful - everything is modern, clean and feels very cozy. A very pleasant atmosphere for relaxation, the wellness equipment is of a high standard. I would definitely come back here.

originalText.text: S návštevou wellnessu som veľmi spokojná. Prostredie je naozaj krásne – všetko je moderné, čisté a pôsobí veľmi útulne. Veľmi píjemná atmosféra na relax, vybavenie wellnessu je na vysokej úrovni. Určite by som sa sem vrátila.

text.text:
My husband and I recently visited Wellness – an hour in the steam sauna and an hour in the jacuzzi. We were very pleased with the atmosphere in the steam sauna: the sauna seems to be "embedded" in the natural terrain, plus the steam creates a wonderful overall feeling of a cave, mystery, and, of course, privacy. The sauna's relaxation area has two loungers, which might be a bit inconvenient if you're relaxing with a group. But that's a minor detail. Some people might appreciate the lack of unnecessary clutter. As for the jacuzzi, I liked the "Italian" atmosphere, the different options for playing with the lighting, etc. Of course, two hours isn't enough... To truly rest and relax, it's best to come here with champagne and fruit and spend at least three hours here.
Otherwise, everything is fine – it's clean, and has everything you need. We'd be happy to come back.

originalText.text:
Были с мужем на днях в Wellness - час парная сауна и час джакузи. Очень порадовала атмосфера в парной сауне: сауна как бы «вделана» в естественный рельеф местности, плюс пар, и создается общее классное ощущение пещеры, таинственности и, конечно, уединения. В зоне отдыха сауны стоят два лежака, что может быть не очень удобно, если отдыхать компанией. Но это мелочь. Кому-то как раз понравится отсутствие лишнего. Что касается джакузи, то там мне понравилась «итальянская» атмосфера, разные возможности поиграть с освещением и тп. Конечно, двух часов мало… Чтобы по-настоящему отдохнуть и расслабиться, сюда лучше прийти с шампанским, фруктами и провести тут не меньше 3 часов))
В остальном всё хорошо - чисто, есть всё необходимое. С удовольствием придём сюда снова)

text.text:
A perfect place to disconnect from the city and fully relax. Everything you need is provided: bathrobes, towels, both regular and rubber slippers, showers, as well as complimentary water and nuts.
The pool and sauna are in excellent condition — clean, comfortable, and peaceful.
I also really appreciated the option to book a massage or order drinks for an even more relaxing experience.
You can truly feel the high level of service and attention to detail. Highly recommended

text.text: It was an absolutely amazing experience! I would highly recommend the Sumatra Sunset Ritual — it's definitely my favourite! It involves gentle exfoliation of the skin with a mask, followed by an incredible massage. The environment is indescribable — you have to experience it for yourself! You approach the therapy area via a bridge over a pond full of carp. It's unbelievable! I highly recommend it!

The best massage and facial I’ve ever had! The therapist was professional and attentive. Finished with a soothing tea—perfect from start to finish.
Highly recommended 👍👍

text.text:
Loved my experience! The space is so calm and beautifully designed, with a relaxing vibe from the moment you walk in. I had the 90-minute Alchymist Massage and it was incredible – super relaxing and exactly what I needed.

The therapist was really professional and made sure I was comfortable the whole time. I walked out feeling completely refreshed and like a new person.

Highly recommend if you're looking for a peaceful escape right in the center of Prague, Malá Strana. I’ll definitely be back!

text.text: This has been absolutely fantastic visit of a hidden gem. I am stunned I haven't discovered Ecsotica Spa earlier. They have fantastic health club with relaxing pool, sauna and steam. The massages are simply amazing. From the moment you will use the stairs to the basement from the hotel reception and suddenly you will are teleported thousands of kilometers to Indonesia where the knowledgeable and welcoming massage therapists takes care of you, your body and mind. With their magical skills they will transfer your body and your consciousness into the tranquillity and outter space. Thank you!!! And I will definitely come back, soon!

displayName.text: AVA Spa at Four Seasons Hotel Prague

text.text:
I had the most incredible experience at Ava Spa at the Four Seasons Prague, and I can’t recommend it enough! I visited on my birthday, and the team went above and beyond to make the day truly unforgettable. From the moment I arrived, I was treated with such warmth and care.

The complimentary foot scrub was absolutely divine, relaxing, rejuvenating, and perfectly executed. But what really made the experience extraordinary was the personal touch: while I was enjoying the vitality pool, they surprised me with a glass of bubbles and a birthday muffin! It was such a thoughtful and joyful gesture that made me feel so appreciated and celebrated.

Every detail, from the serene atmosphere to the impeccable service, reflected genuine hospitality and attention to detail. I left feeling relaxed, pampered, and deeply grateful for the experience.

Thank you, Ava Spa, for making my birthday so special. You’ve truly set the gold standard for luxury and care in Prague!

text.text:
Treatments and masseuses were great.
A delightful moment spent at the spa ♥️

originalText.text:
Treatments and masseuses were great.

text.text: I came with my husband and we had the best massage ever. Loved the staff

originalText.text: I came with my husband and we had the best massage ever. Loved the staff

text.text: Great treatments and relaxing environment!  One of the best massages had.

originalText.text: Great treatments and relaxing environment!  One of the best massages had.

text.text:
Nice location, but despite the rules saying that children are not allowed in the spa and that a quiet and peaceful environment should be maintained (there were children aged 3 and up who were yelling and screaming), they were there and made a terrible racket. It was like a public swimming pool, they were playing football, with masks and fins. YOU CANNOT RELAX IN A SPA LIKE THIS. Furthermore, the mirror didn't light up and the scales in the changing room didn't work.
THEY SHOULD ENFORCE THE RULES IN THIS SPA. Furthermore, I had several expensive treatments, but I couldn't relax in the pool because of rude kids who were yelling and splashing. It felt like a water park, not a spa in a 5-star hotel.
The staff came and went to set up the various beds, but they didn't say anything. My husband mentioned it a few days after booking other expensive treatments, and the manager apologized and told him I could come in the morning when the spa is quieter, but I didn't go because it was the same every time, except once when there were two adults.
I WILL NOT GO BACK.

originalText.text:
Location carina ma , nonostante le regole dicono che non possono entrare i bambini dentro la Spa e che si dovrebbe mantenere un ambiente silenzioso e tranquillo ( c’ erano da 3 anni in su che sbraitavano e urlavano )  , c erano e hanno fatto un casino immondo , sembrava la piscina comunale giocavano a pallone , con maschere e pinne , NON SI PUÒ RILASSARE IN UNA SPA COSÌ , inoltre lo specchio non si accendeva e la bilancia non era funzionante in camerino.
DOVREBBERO FAR RISPETTARE LE REGOLE IN QUESTA SPA, inoltre ho fatto diversi trattamenti costosi, ma non ho potuto rilassarmi in piscina per colpa di mocciosi maleducati che urlavano e schizzavano , sembrava L acquapark non una spa di un 5 stelle hotel.
Il personale entrava e usciva per sistemare i vari lettini, ma non diceva nulla, mio marito lo ha fatto presente i giorni dopo aver prenotato altri trattamenti costosi e il manager si è scusato e gli ha detto che avrei potuto venire la mattina che è più tranquilla la Spa, ma non ci sono andata perchè tutte le volte era sempre la stessa storia, tranne una volta sola che c erano due adulti.
NON CI TORNERÒ.

authorAttributions.displayName: AVA Spa at Four Seasons Hotel Prague

text.text: We did the 3-hour fitness sauna package, followed by lunch at the attached steak restaurant (check my other reviews), followed by the rooftop jacuzzi with Prosecco... M Spa is really nice... the fitness room is super small but they have a really cool cable weight lifting machine you can use to get a pretty good workout on... the sauna and steam room were both nice and the locker rooms and showers were clean and nice... the lady working at the front desk was really sweet and made our visit very pleasant.... the. Only complaint I have is that the fitness room was very clean... lots of hair on the floor and very noticed when doing ab workout on mats... the rooftop jacuzzi was fun and private—even though there are houses all around.

text.text: What a relaxing time!  I am 5 days out from lower back surgery and could barely make it.  In the way back, I had a much easier time.  Thanks to the jacuzzi and spa time.

text.text: Wonderful massage by Ena. Extremely professional. Location is easy to find and the spa was immaculately clean. I would ignore the tourist massage traps and go straight here.

text.text: A fabulous experience with clean and calm facilities - the massage with Inna was fantastic. Will be back!

text.text: The first visit was great, we were excited, but for the second time in about 2 years, we were disappointed, the bathrobes were horrible, the refreshments were very poor, the seating in the rest area was dirty and disgusting. The last time we visited this place, it's a shame that the services have deteriorated so much and it's not cheap.

text.text:
From the very moment we arrived, we were greeted by exceptionally warm and attentive staff at the entrance, who confirmed our names and kindly escorted us to the Fairmont Gold Lounge for check-in. It was a level of welcome that I had not experienced even at the Forbes 5-Star Four Seasons Prague.

The Fairmont Gold Lounge team was truly outstanding—genuinely kind, professional, and thoughtful. The entire check-in process was seamless and unhurried. What impressed me most was how respectfully the staff handled our room selection, patiently waiting without any pressure and always prioritizing our comfort. Among them, Marcos, who personally escorted us, was so remarkably kind and attentive that I felt compelled to ask for his name—an experience I will not forget.

Upgrading to a Fairmont Gold room was absolutely the right decision. The immaculate condition of the room, with perfectly clean bedding and towels, along with flawless housekeeping, made the space feel welcoming and comfortable from the very first moment we entered.

This hotel truly stands out as one of the few in the world that not only remembers the primary guest, but also takes the time to recognize and care for accompanying guests by name. Throughout our stay, we were fortunate to encounter only sincere and thoughtful service—from the friendly gentleman at the gym who remembered us from our second visit, to the doormen at the entrance who kindly assisted and looked after us all the way until our departure for the airport.

Lastly, I would like to express my heartfelt thanks to Katka for the warm farewell and the beautiful welcome letter. I will cherish it and keep it as a precious memory of our stay.

If I have the opportunity to visit Prague again, Fairmont Golden Prague will undoubtedly be my first choice.

The Fairmont Gold Lounge team was truly outstanding—genuinely kind, professional, and thoughtful. The entire check-in process was seamless and unhurried. text.text:
We recently spent three nights at the Fairmont Prague and left feeling really happy with our choice. We booked a Gold Room, and it was worth every penny for the added service and comfort.
A special shout-out to Kate at the Gold Lounge; she was incredibly welcoming and took such good care of us throughout our stay. It’s those personal touches that make a hotel feel like home.

The Highlights:

Dining: We had the course menu at Zlata Restaurant. The food was excellent, but the panoramic views of Prague really stole the show. Highly recommended for a romantic dinner!

The Spa: Very well maintained with a variety of saunas and a surprisingly large pool.

Room: Comfortable, modern, and the Gold Lounge access made a huge difference for breakfast and evening drinks.
One minor suggestion: While the spa is great, we did find it a bit difficult to find a spot to relax—some extra space for laying down/loungers would be a great addition to the pool area.

Overall, a wonderful stay and we would definitely return!

text.text:
We spent a wonderful couple’s weekend at the Fairmont. The hotel is recently renovated and feels very new, modern, and high-end. Our room was spotless with a beautiful view of the Vltava river.
The location is excellent—situated in the Old Town just a 5-minute walk from the main square, which keeps it peaceful while staying central. The fitness center is amazing and very well-equipped, and the staff were extremely professional and kind. We also really appreciated the room upgrade through the Accor loyalty program. Highly recommended!

originalText.text:

The location is excellent—situated in the Old Town just a 5-minute walk from the main square, which keeps it peaceful while staying central. The fitness center is amazing and very well-equipped, and the staff were extremely professional and kind. We also really appreciated the room upgrade through the Accor loyalty program. Highly recommended!

text.text:
The experience was highly satisfactory. The Fairmont Gold lounge stands out as one of the finest I have encountered. I was somewhat surprised to find that the food selection in the lounge mirrored that of Kafka's restaurant, as I had anticipated a more extensive and sophisticated culinary offering at Kafka.

The spa facilities are impressive and well-maintained. However, the absence of a "no swimsuit policy" in the saunas was a significant disappointment and detracted from the overall experience. I recommend implementing a no-swimsuit policy, particularly for the saunas located within the changing rooms.

Despite these observations, I would certainly consider returning for another stay. The recent renovations have been executed with exceptional quality, and the service provided was both attentive and outstanding.

text.text:
Spectacular ! Perfect location. Beautiful property, high-tech elegant rooms. Art works everyone, the hotel feels like an art gallery. The spa proposes modern facilities, ideal after a day of sight-seeing.
The buffet breakfast is a must, with view to the castle. The rooftop bar serves delicious Asian food. The lobby bar is refined and cocooning, I recommend having tea served with honey from the hotel's honeycombs.
The staff is perfect, they will fulfill any request. Fairmont is a place to remember and to wish to come back.

The staff is perfect, they will fulfill any request. Fairmont is a place to remember and to wish to come back.

editorialSummary.text: Large indoor venue with several slides, a lazy river, children's splash area & 50m pool.

text.text: If you’re an endurance athlete, especially a swimmer, this review’s for you. Not so much for families or kids. I’ve swum here twice, so here’s what I found. Getting there by public transport? Totally doable, but be ready for a 20-minute walk from the station. The price is a win: 180 Czech krones for 75 minutes. The place is well-kept, with a cafeteria, showers, changing rooms, and lockers. You even get a key. Now, here’s where it gets interesting. The pool is 50 meters long, shallow on one end (1.36m), deep on the other (2.2m), and there’s a big clock on the wall for timing your sets. But most days, about 10 meters of the pool is roped off for water polo athletes. Watching them train is cool, but if you’re swimming laps, you’ll hit a buoy line instead of a wall. Flip turns? Open water turns? Not so simple. There aren’t any lane lines, so everyone just kind of does their own thing. Once, a bunch of kids jumped in and started playing, and the lifeguards didn’t seem to mind. So, if you’re thinking of training here, maybe keep it as your plan B. It’s fine for easy, technical swims, but not for serious sets. Want more swim spots in Europe? Check out @triathlontrips on Instagram, Facebook, or the website.

The place is well-kept, with a cafeteria, showers, changing rooms, and lockers. You even get a key. Now, here’s where it gets interesting. The pool is 50 meters long, shallow on one end (1.36m), deep on the other (2.2m), and there’s a big clock on the wall for timing your sets. But most days, about 10 meters of the pool is roped off for water polo athletes. Watching them train is cool, but if you’re swimming laps, you’ll hit a buoy line instead of a wall. Flip turns? Open water turns? Not so simple. There aren’t any lane lines, so everyone just kind of does their own thing. Once, a bunch of kids jumped in and started playing, and the lifeguards didn’t seem to mind. So, if you’re thinking of training here, maybe keep it as your plan B. It’s fine for easy, technical swims, but not for serious sets. Want more swim spots in Europe? Check out @triathlontrips on Instagram, Facebook, or the website.

text.text: The place is great and clean. however, the water is in my personal opinion way too cold for “normal” slow swimming (except if you go there for medium-hard training or competitions)...

text.text: Such a nice aqua park, loads of pool with different depths and lots of slides.

originalText.text: Such a nice aqua park, loads of pool with different depths and lots of slides.

text.text:
My son really enjoys this place but recently they have raised prices and introduced extremely high parking fees. There is nowhere else to park so you must pay it. I actually thought there must be a mistake when I saw 200czk for 90 mins!!! was a mistake but but it cost us 200czk for 90 mins of parking, plus 500czk for me and my son in the pools for 90 mins. It's nice but the prices are just gouging (500czk for the park + 200 parking = 700 for two of us for 90 mins), so I think that is our last trip to Sutka.

Update: they commented below to imply that I didn’t follow some instructions. I can assure you that I went especially to the cash desk to ask about the parking and I was told to pay on my way out. This is a grift to scam people out of the extra 200czk, further highlighted by propaganda responses to negative reviews. Avoid this place if you enjoy value for money and honesty.
